items.headline: Iran's military vows to continue war until US, Israel 'regret' aggression
items.summary: The senior spokesman for Iran's Armed Forces says the country will continue the war as long as it makes the United States and the Israeli regime "regret" their illegal act of aggression.

items.headline: 'Regime change' in the Strait of Hormuz sees 20,000 sailors stranded at sea
items.summary: Interviews and first-hand accounts from sailors stranded in the Persian Gulf — under threat of drone attacks, missiles, and exploding boats — reveal an increasingly dire humanitarian crisis unfolding.

items.summary: Pakistan's Deputy PM Ishaq Dar visits China after Islamabad talks to broker negotiations between the US and Iran.

items.headline: Russia Oil Revenues Halved in March Before War Boost
items.summary: Russian producers paid 494.9 billion rubles ($6.18 billion) in oil taxes last month, down 48 percent year on year, according to Bloomberg calculations based on Finance Ministry data released Friday.

items.headline: Russia’s Oil Revenues Halved in March Before Iran War Boost
items.summary: Russia’s oil tax revenue in March dropped by nearly half compared to a year earlier, highlighting the Kremlin’s financial strain just before the Middle East war delivered an unexpected boost to Moscow’s earnings.

items.headline: Oil prices surge while Asian share prices rise moderately
items.summary: Oil prices spike amid Iran war concerns as Asian markets show modest gains in cautious trading.

items.headline: Deterrent or danger: After Iran, are Saudi Arabia and Turkey next in line for nuclear weapons?
items.summary: The nuclear threat in the Middle East is growing due to the Iran war, and not just because nuclear sites in Iran and Israel are being attacked. Other countries may decide they're safer with a nuclear weapon too.

items.headline: HMA Moscow Nigel Casey's interview on UK-Russia relations

items.headline: One Crew Member Rescued After American Jet Shot Down In Iran, US Official Tells RFE/RL
items.summary: A US crew member has been rescued by American forces after a jet fighter was shot down over Iran, a US official told RFE/RL, and the search is ongoing for a second, in the first known loss of an American plane to hostile fire since the war began.

items.headline: Tehran Hits Gulf Energy Sites, Trump Warns US 'Hasn't Even Started' To Destroy Iran
items.summary: Iran launched attacks across the Middle East on April 3, setting parts of a major Kuwaiti oil refinery ablaze and triggering air defense responses across the Gulf, as the war with the United States and Israel neared the end of its fifth week.

items.headline: Rescue Mission Races To Find Crew Member Of US Jet Downed Over Iran
items.summary: Rescue teams continue to search early on April 4 for a US crew member missing after their fighter jet was brought down over Iran, one of two American forces planes reportedly knocked out of the sky in the region a day earlier.

items.headline: Satellite images begin to show damage wrought by Iran war
items.summary: Satellite images are emerging, offering a view of the Iran war's damage. Ships are seen burning in an Iranian port. Military bases in the region show destroyed buildings. Information on the war's toll has been limited. High-resolution images from various firm…

items.headline: What to know about U.S. shifting targets, new deployments and diplomatic double game as conflict enters Day 30?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran that began with targeted "decapitation" strikes on Feb. 28 has spiralled into a multi-front regional war with no end in sight.

items.headline: Iran hardliners call for full nuclear armament following US-Israel attacks during negotiations
items.summary: Iranian parliament hardliners are demanding advancement to full nuclear weapons capability after US-Israeli attacks occurred while nuclear negotiations were ongoing. The IAEA reported concerns about Iran's continued stockpile of 440 kg of uranium enriched to 60%.

dimension_signal.rationale: Launched military strikes against Iran while nuclear negotiations were actively ongoing

items.headline: IAEA cannot access bombed Iranian nuclear sites, derailing safeguards obligations
items.summary: The International Atomic Energy Agency reports inability to access any of the bombed Iranian nuclear facilities or account for nuclear material since US-Israeli strikes began. This constitutes a violation of Iran's safeguards obligations.

items.headline: Xi Jinping purges up to 100 senior PLA officers including Central Military Commission members
items.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping has detained or disappeared approximately 100 senior military officers by early 2026, including five of six Central Military Commission members. The purge likely leaves China's military command in disarray.

dimension_signal.rationale: Massive military purge indicates deep paranoia about loyalty and control within PLA ranks

items.headline: Pakistani Forces deployed Pakistani-origin Zainebiyoun militia to Iran's Qom city
items.summary: Pakistani-origin Shiite militia Zainebiyoun forces have been documented operating in Iran's Qom city alongside Afghan Fatemiyoun forces to strengthen regime control in urban areas during the ongoing conflict.

items.headline: IMF imposes 11 new conditions on Pakistan as geopolitical tensions threaten bailout
items.summary: International Monetary Fund has imposed 11 additional conditions on Pakistan's $7 billion bailout package and warned that escalating tensions with India may jeopardize fiscal and structural reform targets.

items.headline: Over 232,500 Afghans returned from Pakistan and Iran since January amid border hostilities
items.summary: UNHCR reports 146,206 Afghans returned from Pakistan and 86,253 from Iran in early 2026, with thousands displaced in Kunar and Nangarhar provinces due to Afghanistan-Pakistan hostilities. Torkham border crossing currently closed.

items.headline: Netanyahu coalition advances bill to repeal fraud and breach of trust criminal charges
items.summary: Israeli coalition lawmakers are advancing legislation to eliminate the criminal offense of fraud and breach of trust from law books, charges that Netanyahu faces in all three corruption cases. Opposition calls it a 'full-fledged coup.'

dimension_signal.rationale: Attempting to eliminate criminal charges he faces through retroactive legislation

items.headline: Trump negotiator Steve Witkoff shows limited understanding of uranium enrichment process
items.summary: Arms control experts express concerns about US negotiator Steve Witkoff's technical knowledge after he confused nuclear reactors with centrifuge facilities and was surprised by Iran's documented uranium stockpile levels during February talks.

dimension_signal.rationale: Deploying negotiators with insufficient technical expertise for nuclear negotiations

items.headline: China deploys PLA Cyberspace Force to South China Sea artificial island on combat readiness
items.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping's Lunar New Year military address revealed PLA Cyberspace Force deployment to Fiery Cross Reef in disputed Spratly Islands, with commanders reporting 'combat readiness duty' status.
